close_reason: All child tasks are closed and landed on feat/more-om.
description:
Practical backlog summary anchor: **Tier 1 → Continuous/background observation with next-turn-only publication**.

Backlog detail: `packages/pi-om-extension/docs/mastra-parity-backlog.md` → **Tier 1 / section 2. Continuous/background observation, but only next-turn publication**.

Implementation context:
- current lifecycle is documented in `packages/pi-om-extension/docs/observation-timing-deviation.md`
- hooks are wired in `packages/pi-om-extension/src/index.ts`
- observation state and cursor logic live in `src/engine.ts`, `src/types.ts`, and `src/state.ts`
- today observation runs in `agent_end`, injection happens in `before_agent_start`, and pruning happens in `context`

What this epic should establish:
- staged/draft observation state that can accumulate during long runs
- publication only at turn boundaries
- no mid-turn system-prompt mutation and no pruning cursor drift ahead of published observations

Useful references:
- backlog doc section 2 plus linked Mastra refs (`buffering-coordinator.ts`, `async-buffer.ts`, `mid-loop-observation.test.ts`)
- local extension/integration tests around lifecycle timing in `src/__tests__`

Non-goal: do not recreate Mastra/OpenCode mid-turn swap behavior in pi.
title: Continuous/background observation with next-turn-only publication
